sections.subtitle: Current Form and Recent Performances
sections.text: Real Betis come into this match in solid form, having secured three wins in their last five games, including a confidence-boosting away victory against Mallorca. Rayo Vallecano, however, have struggled recently, managing only one win in their last five matches, with losses against stronger sides like Real Madrid and Celta Vigo highlighting defensive vulnerabilities. Betis’ attacking consistency and home advantage give them an edge in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Real Betis and Rayo Vallecano have been tightly contested, with both ending in draws. The most recent clash saw neither side finding the breakthrough, while the earlier meeting produced an entertaining stalemate. These results suggest a closely fought battle could be on the cards again.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Key Insights
sections.text: Real Betis, sitting 5th in the league with a strong offensive output, rank among the top teams for goals scored and big chances created. Rayo Vallecano, languishing in 17th, have struggled to convert chances and often concede first, as seen in recent games. Betis’ superior league position and statistical dominance make them the favorites.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook and Key Players
sections.text: Betis will rely on in-form players like Cucho Hernández and Antony to drive their attack, while their defensive solidity at home could frustrate Rayo’s efforts. Rayo Vallecano’s Jorge de Frutos poses a threat on the counter, but their lack of consistency in front of goal might hinder them. Expect Betis to control possession and create more scoring opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Real Betis and Rayo Vallecano at Estadio de la Cartuja ended with a 1-1 scoreline. Both teams showed intent, with Real Betis striking first through Cédric Bakambu in the 16th minute, only for Rayo Vallecano to equalize late in the half via Isi Palazón at the 42nd minute. The game has been competitive, with each side creating opportunities and displaying moments of quality in the final third. Both teams have had their share of possession, though Rayo Vallecano held a slight edge in controlling the ball. Discipline has been an issue, with each team picking up a couple of yellow cards.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attacking momentum in the first half saw significant shifts between the two sides. Real Betis started with early dominance, pushing forward with consistent attacking spells during the opening stages and around the time of their goal. Their pressure was notable in the middle of the half as well, maintaining a threat in the opposition's penalty area. However, Rayo Vallecano grew into the game, showing increased intent as the half progressed, especially with sustained attacking momentum leading up to their equalizer. Their late surge in the closing minutes of the half suggests they ended on a high note and could carry that energy into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the team statistics, Real Betis created more big chances in the first half, though they missed a key opportunity to extend their lead. Their entries into the penalty area and final third were slightly less frequent compared to Rayo Vallecano, who showed greater success in winning duels, particularly in the air. Rayo Vallecano also had more shots on target, indicating a sharper edge in front of goal despite lower expected goals. Both teams earned an equal number of corner kicks, suggesting set-pieces could play a role in the second half. Defensively, both sides have vulnerabilities, as seen in the goals conceded and possession lost in dangerous areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Cédric Bakambu has been the standout for Real Betis, not only scoring the opener but also creating a big chance and delivering key passes. His presence in attack will be crucial for the home side in the second half. For Rayo Vallecano, Isi Palazón was clinical with his goal and showed creativity with key passes, while Andrei Rațiu contributed significantly with an assist and a high impact in the final third. These players are likely to be focal points for their respective teams as they push for a winner.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an open contest, with both teams having shown they can exploit defensive lapses. Real Betis will likely look to capitalize on their ability to create big chances, potentially relying on Bakambu to lead the line. Rayo Vallecano, buoyed by their late equalizer, may push for another goal with Palazón being a constant threat. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, especially in midfield, where winning duels and maintaining possession will be key. Given the attacking intent from both sides and the statistical balance, the match could see more goals before the final whistle.
